name: I hurt my back lifting boxes at work yesterday, what do I do right now?
description: Report to your supervisor now, get medical help, and document everything—delays risk denial[1][2].

mainEntity.name: I hurt my back lifting boxes at work yesterday, what do I do right now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to your supervisor now, get medical help, and document everything—delays risk denial[1][2].
acceptedAnswer.description: **Report the injury to your supervisor immediately, ideally within 24 hours, and seek medical attention.** In most states, notify your employer in writing as soon as possible to start the workers' compensation process[1][2]. Delays can jeopardize your claim. **Key factors:** Timely reporting preserves benefits like medical coverage and wage replacement; document everything including witnesses. **Practical guidance:** Get a doctor's note linking the injury to work, avoid giving recorded statements to insurance adjusters without advice, and track all expenses. Consult a workers' comp attorney if unsure. **Utah and Idaho emphasize prompt action to avoid denials[1][2].**
